commit.message:
docs(examples): add deployment examples for docker-compose, kubernetes, and systemd (#1693)

Manael lacks reference deployment configurations, making it harder for
users to adopt best-practice architectures (especially with a caching
frontend). Adds an `examples/` directory with ready-to-use
configurations for three environments.

## docker-compose (`examples/docker-compose/`)
- **`compose.yaml`**: Nginx (port 80) fronting Manael (port 8080)
- **`nginx.conf`**: Nginx cache with an explicit `proxy_cache_key` built
from only relevant query params — prevents cache fragmentation from
parameter ordering or tracking params like `utm_source`:
  ```nginx
proxy_cache_key
"$scheme$proxy_host$uri?w=$arg_w&h=$arg_h&q=$arg_q&fit=$arg_fit";
  ```
- **`.env.example`**: All supported env vars with inline docs

## Kubernetes (`examples/kubernetes/`)
- **`deployment.yaml`**: 2-replica deployment with resource limits,
liveness/readiness probes on `/_health`, and a hardened security context
(non-root, read-only root filesystem, all capabilities dropped, writable
`/tmp` via `emptyDir`)
- **`service.yaml`**: ClusterIP service for cluster-internal routing

## systemd (`examples/systemd/`)
- **`manael.service`**: Unit file with `Restart=on-failure`, non-root
`manael` user, and hardening directives (`NoNewPrivileges`,
`ProtectSystem=strict`, `PrivateTmp`, etc.)
- **`manael.env`**: All supported env vars, loaded via
`EnvironmentFile=`

commit.message:
fix: robust routing — no more 404s on GitHub Pages

Three-pronged routing fix:

1. navigate() always uses hash mode
   Removed broken History/Hash mode detection that sometimes fell into
   pushState mode, causing real path changes that 404 on static hosts.
   Now always does location.set_hash('#/path').

2. 404.html SPA redirect
   GitHub Pages serves this for any unknown path. Redirects
   /Oxide/components/button → /Oxide/#/components/button so
   direct links, bookmarks, and refreshes all work.

3. Proper href attributes on all nav links
   Links now have href='#/path' so right-click → open-in-new-tab
   works correctly. Click handler calls preventDefault + navigate().

4. index.html self-redirect
   Strips /index.html from URL if present, redirecting to clean /.

commit.message:
fix: rate limit not detected in idle handler and pending-message paths

Three bugs caused rate limits to be silently swallowed, leaving the loop
to enter SLEEPING state instead of showing the rate limit banner.

Bug 1 – Launcher message ordering: If the SDK emits rate_limit_event
AFTER the result message, the previous fix (checking rateLimitText when
processing result) didn't fire. Fix: in the rate_limit_event handler,
immediately set isError=true and errorMessage=rateLimitText so the rate
limit text is always in result.error regardless of message order.

Bug 2 – Idle handler paths (Id / Superego / Ego): All three roles silently
swallowed rate-limit errors — Id.generateDrives() returned [], Superego
rejected all proposals, Ego.respondToMessage() returned null — causing
IdleHandler to return 'no_goals'/'all_rejected' and the orchestrator to
call enterSleep() instead of backing off.

Fix: add RateLimitError and isRateLimitText() helpers. Id, Superego, and
Ego now throw RateLimitError when result.error contains rate limit text.
The orchestrator re-throws it from the respondToMessage catch block. A
top-level try/catch in runLoop() catches RateLimitError from any path
(cycle, idle handler, message response) and delegates to the new
applyRateLimitBackoff() helper, which was extracted from the existing
dispatch-path rate limit handling to avoid duplication.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: numeric route ID compression for O(1) dispatch (#1313)

Add NumericRouteTable — a flat array-indexed dispatch table that
assigns deterministic sequential IDs to all routes (sorted by key
for stability across restarts):

- Build(): collects routes, sorts by key, assigns IDs 0..N-1
- TryLookup(int routeId): O(1) array index, zero string comparison
- ParseRouteId(): branchless ASCII digit parser that stops at
  first non-digit character

Dispatch fast path in BareMetalWebServer.RequestHandler:
- If path starts with /<digit>, parse route ID and dispatch via
  array lookup before the jump table/prefix router stages
- Falls back to existing routing for non-numeric paths
- Full auth/logging integration on the numeric path

Metadata export endpoint: GET /bmw/routes returns JSON array of
RouteId → Verb → Path mappings for client library integration.

25 new tests covering: table building, deterministic ID assignment,
lookup (valid/invalid/out-of-range/empty), rebuild, parser
(digits, non-digits, trailing slash/query/space, edge cases).

Updated RegisterMonitoringRoutes route count assertion (5→6).

Closes #1311

commit.message:
Fix custom checkbox detection in nested label structures (#540)

* Fix #539: Use closest('LABEL') to detect ancestor labels for custom checkboxes

Replace elem.parentElement.tagName == 'LABEL' with elem.closest('LABEL')

commit.message:
perf: property cache, cheap_clone, Smi fast-paths for bitwise/div/mod (#448)

* perf(property_map): add inline name cache for fast repeated lookups

Add a 4-entry FNV-1a hash-based inline cache to PropertyMap that sits in
front of the HashMap index. On repeated lookups of the same property names
(the common case in tight JS loops), the cache returns the slot index via
a cheap linear scan of 4 hash entries, avoiding the full SipHash + probe
overhead of HashMap::get.

The cache uses Cell-based interior mutability so read-only (&self) methods
like get(), contains_key(), and get_with_attrs() can populate the cache
transparently. The cache is automatically invalidated on remove() since
swap-remove changes slot indices.

PartialEq is now implemented manually to exclude cache state from equality
comparisons.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* perf(interpreter): reduce JsValue clone overhead in hot dispatch paths

- Add JsValue::cheap_clone() that does bitwise copy for scalar variants
  (Smi, HeapNumber, Boolean, Undefined, Null, etc.) without going
  through the derived Clone which may touch Rc refcounts unnecessarily.

- Add InterpreterFrame::copy_reg() for Mov opcode, avoiding the
  intermediate read_reg/write_reg borrow dance and skipping the copy
  when src == dst.

- Switch Ldar, Star, Mov, Add, Sub, Mul slow paths, and TestEqual/
  TestNotEqual to use cheap_clone() instead of clone().

- Add Smi fast paths to Div, Mod, Exp, BitwiseOr, BitwiseXor,
  BitwiseAnd, ShiftLeft, ShiftRight, and ShiftRightLogical handlers.
  These return early without cloning the register value when both
  operands are Smi (Copy semantics via pattern match).

- Add #[inline] to JsValue::to_number() (hot conversion path).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add String.prototype.trimLeft/trimRight legacy aliases

Add trimLeft as alias for trimStart and trimRight as alias for trimEnd
per ECMAScript Annex B §B.2.3.1 and §B.2.3.2.

All other conformance items (optional chaining, nullish coalescing,
logical assignment operators, numeric separators, globalThis,
Array.prototype.flat/flatMap, Object.hasOwn, Error.cause, and at()
on String/Array/TypedArray) were already implemented.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: BMW protocol descriptor and SDK generator (#1325) (#1336)

BmwProtocolDescriptor.cs — deterministic protocol descriptor:
- Enumerates all registered routes, computes opcodes via (method<<11)|route
- RouteDescriptor: name, opcode, method, routeOrdinal, entity, params, permission
- EntityDescriptor: groups all opcodes for an entity slug
- Lookup tables: by name, by opcode, by entity
- ToJson(): cached JSON serialization of full protocol spec
- GenerateJsSdk(): virtual SDK with WebSocket binary transport
- GenerateCliReference(): CLI command mappings

Endpoints registered in EnsureBinaryTransport():
- GET /bmw/protocol — full protocol descriptor JSON
- GET /bmw/sdk.js — auto-generated JS SDK module
- GET /bmw/cli — CLI command reference

The descriptor is the single shared contract between server and all clients.
Opcode generation is deterministic; route ordinals are stable across restarts.

Tests: 17 new tests covering opcode computation, entity grouping, parameter
extraction, JSON validity, transport constants, SDK generation, CLI output,
caching, and edge cases.

fix: character select input + CI import step (v3) (#120)

ROOT CAUSE 1 — _unhandled_input never fires in built .exe:
Something in the scene tree consumes input before _unhandled_input
gets it. Switched to _input which fires FIRST in Godot's input
pipeline, before any GUI node processing. Also added explicit
set_process_input(true) in _ready() as a safeguard.

ROOT CAUSE 2 — CI export skips project import:
Godot 4 headless export needs the project imported first so scripts
attach properly. Added a --headless --import step before export.

commit.message:
[Repo Assist] Add Series.empty and Frame.empty module functions (closes #56) (#607)

🤖 *This is an automated PR from Repo Assist.*

Implements the `Frame.empty` and `Series.empty` values requested in #56.

## What's Added

### `Series.empty`
```fsharp
let empty<'K, 'V when 'K : equality> : Series<'K, 'V>
```
Returns an empty series with no keys or values.

### `Frame.empty`
```fsharp
let empty<'R, 'C when 'R : equality and 'C : equality> : Frame<'R, 'C>
```
Returns an empty data frame with no rows or columns.

## Example

```fsharp
let s : Series(int, float) = Series.empty
let df : Frame(int, string) = Frame.empty

// Useful as a starting point for fold-style frame construction:
let df =
    columns
    |> List.fold (fun acc (name, data) -> acc |> Frame.addCol name data) Frame.empty
```

## Design Notes

- Both are typed `let` bindings with explicit type parameters — the F#
compiler infers the concrete type from usage context, exactly as callers
expect.
- The implementation delegates to the existing `Series([], [])` /
`Frame([], [])` constructors so there is no duplication of logic.

## Test Status

✅ All 490 tests pass (5 new tests added covering empty series and frame
creation, merging, and column addition).

[Repo Assist] Add Series.diffDate and Series.diffDateOffset for DateTime/DateTimeOffset differences (#592)

🤖 *This PR was created by Repo Assist, an automated AI assistant.*

## Summary

Adds `Series.diffDate` and `Series.diffDateOffset` functions that
compute the `TimeSpan` difference between consecutive `DateTime` /
`DateTimeOffset` values in a series — addressing the feature request in
#565.

## Motivation

The existing `Series.diff` uses an F# inline type-constrained
implementation requiring homogeneous subtraction (`T - T = T`).
`DateTime - DateTime = TimeSpan` is heterogeneous, so `Series.diff`
cannot be used for timestamp series and would raise a runtime type error
if attempted.

## Implementation

The new functions use `shift` + `zipInto` internally:

````fsharp
let diffDate offset (series:Series<'K, DateTime>) : Series<'K, TimeSpan> =
    (series, series |> shift offset) ||> zipInto (fun curr prev -> curr - prev)
```

This produces exactly the same semantics as numeric `diff`:

```
result[k] = series[k] - series[k - offset]
````

For `offset = 1`, this gives the elapsed time since the previous
observation — useful for detecting periodicity, irregular intervals,
gaps, etc.

### New API surface

| F# | C# | Returns |
|---|---|---|
| `Series.diffDate offset series` | `series.Diff(offset)` | `Series<'K,
TimeSpan>` |
| `Series.diffDateOffset offset series` | `series.Diff(offset)` |
`Series<'K, TimeSpan>` |

## Usage example

```fsharp
// Detect time intervals between observations
let timestamps =
    series [ 1 => DateTime(2024,1,1,0,0,0)
             2 => DateTime(2024,1,1,1,0,0)   // +1h
             3 => DateTime(2024,1,1,1,30,0)  // +30m
             4 => DateTime(2024,1,1,3,0,0) ] // +1.5h

timestamps |> Series.diffDate 1
// Series(int, TimeSpan):
//   2 -> 01:00:00
//   3 -> 00:30:00
//   4 -> 01:30:00
```

## Trade-offs

- Adds two new exported functions; naming (`diffDate` /
`diffDateOffset`) mirrors the existing `diff` naming convention.
- Implementation is slightly less efficient than the low-level
index-shift path used by numeric `diff`, but the difference is
negligible for typical use cases and the clarity is worth it.

## Test Status

**Build**: ✅ `dotnet build src/Deedle/Deedle.fsproj -c Release` —
succeeded, 0 errors.

**Tests**: ✅ 4 new tests added in `tests/Deedle.Tests/Series.fs`; all
pass. Full suite: 467 passing (1 pre-existing failure in "Can expand
properties based on runtime type information" — unrelated to this PR).

Note: `dotnet fake build` has an infrastructure issue in this
environment (FAKE TypeInitializationException); the `dotnet build` /
`dotnet test` paths were used instead.

Closes #565

commit.message:
feat: store pin images permanently in Azure Blob Storage

Discord CDN URLs expire (~24h). This replaces the interim refresh-on-read
approach with permanent blob storage using managed identities — no shared
keys, no expiring URLs in Cosmos DB.

Architecture:
- Bot uploads images to Azure Blob Storage at pin creation time using
  DefaultAzureCredential (Storage Blob Data Contributor role)
- Cosmos DB stores the permanent base blob URL (no SAS params)
- getPins API generates User Delegation SAS URLs (7-day TTL) at read time
  using DefaultAzureCredential (Storage Blob Delegator + Data Reader roles)
- Frontend receives SAS URLs; browser loads images directly from blob
- Local dev: if AZURE_STORAGE_ACCOUNT_NAME not set, Discord URL is used
  unchanged (no blob upload, no SAS generation)

Infrastructure changes:
- functions.bicep: add private pin-images blob container; output
  storageAccountName; remove DISCORD_BOT_TOKEN (no longer needed in API);
  add AZURE_STORAGE_ACCOUNT_NAME + IMAGE_STORAGE_CONTAINER app settings
- containerapp.bicep: add AZURE_STORAGE_ACCOUNT_NAME + IMAGE_STORAGE_CONTAINER
- main.bicep: pass storageAccountName to container app; add role assignments
  for bot (Blob Data Contributor) and API (Blob Delegator + Blob Data Reader)
- Production: applied via az CLI (container created, roles assigned, env vars set)

Bot changes:
- storage.ts (new): uploadImageToBlob() — downloads from Discord, uploads to
  blob, returns permanent base URL; fallback to Discord URL on any error
- pinProcessor.ts: call uploadImageToBlob after successful geocoding

API changes:
- blobSas.ts (new): generateSasUrl() — User Delegation SAS with 7-day TTL;
  delegation key cached in memory to minimise getUserDelegationKey calls
- getPins.ts: replace discordRefresh with generateSasUrl; Discord CDN URLs
  (pre-migration pins) pass through unchanged until migration runs
- discordRefresh.ts: deleted

Frontend:
- staticwebapp.config.json: add blob storage domain to img-src CSP

Migration:
- bot/scripts/migrateImageUrls.ts (new): one-time script to backfill all
  existing pins; fetches fresh Discord attachment URLs via bot token, uploads
  to blob, upserts Cosmos DB; safe to re-run

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix: マルチユーザ対応 (#3) (#4)

* Fix: マルチユーザ対応 (#3)

## 分析結果

このissueは、現在の単一ユーザー前提のシステムをマルチユーザー対応に拡張する大規模な機能追加の提案です。実装方針が非常に詳細に記載されており、以下の要素が明確です：

- **目的**: 複数ユーザーが各自のSNSアカウントを設定・管理できるようにする
- **技術的アプローチ**: Web Crypto APIによる認証情報の暗号化、D1データベースでの管理
- **セキュリティ考慮**: マスターキーをSecret管理、AES-GCMによる暗号化
- **具体的な実装コード**: 暗号化/復号化のユーティリティ関数の実装例が提示済み
- **移行方針**: 既存データの移行は不要、新規登録日時以降のポストのみ対象

ユーザーからの追加コメントで技術的な詳細設計まで提示されており、実装に必要な情報は十分に揃っています。

## 提案ラベル

- enhancement

## 対応方針

### 1. データベース設計（D1スキーマ）
- usersテーブル: ユーザーID、メールアドレス、パスワードハッシュ、登録日時
- social_accountsテーブル

Closes #3

- worker.js: POST /api/change-password エンドポイントを追加
- html.js: 設定画面をマルチカラムレイアウトに変更
  - 左カラム: 投稿設定（Bluesky / Misskey.io / Threads）
  - 右カラム: パスワード変更フォーム
  - ページ上部にヘッダーとログアウトボタンを配置
  - 700px以下でシングルカラムにフォールバック

  - GET /auth/threads: 認可開始（state生成・KV保存・認可ページへリダイレクト）

* security: harden auth with PBKDF2, httpOnly cookies, CSRF, rate limiting, full session cleanup

- crypto.js: PBKDF2+salt password hashing (600k iterations, OWASP recommendation)
  with constant-time comparison to prevent timing attacks
- auth.js: SHA-256 → PBKDF2 auto-migration on next login; all sessions deleted
  on account deletion using KV prefix listing
- worker.js: httpOnly Secure SameSite=Strict cookies replace localStorage tokens;
  Origin header validation on all POST /api/* endpoints (CSRF protection);
  rate limiting on login/register (5 attempts / 15 min per IP);
  POST /auth/threads/start to avoid session token in URL
- html.js: remove localStorage and Authorization headers; use credentials: same-origin
- settings.js: fix getPublicSettings SQL to include encrypted token columns
- migrations/0003: add password_hash_version and password_salt columns

* fix: remove duplicate headers key in logout response, add login try/catch

* fix: reduce PBKDF2 iterations to 100000 (Cloudflare Workers limit)

Cloudflare Workers' Web Crypto API does not support iteration counts
above 100,000 for PBKDF2. Reduced from 600,000 to 100,000.

* fix: use reason.indexedAt for repost createdAt to avoid date filter exclusion

Reposts were being filtered out when the original post was older than
the 'since' window (24h). Now uses reason.indexedAt (the time the user
reposted) instead of record.createdAt (original post creation time).
Also adds author handle comparison as fallback repost detection.

* fix: preserve repost type/url in queue message to survive fetchBlueskyPostByUri

fetchBlueskyPostByUri uses getPosts API which has no 'reason' field,
so type and repostUrl were lost when re-fetching. Now store postType
and repostUrl in the queue message and restore them after re-fetch.

commit.message:
[Repo Assist] Fix Finance.ewmVol computing RMS instead of standard deviation (issue #555) (#593)

🤖 *This PR was created by Repo Assist, an automated AI assistant.*

## Summary

Fixes `Finance.ewmVol` returning values near the series mean instead of
the standard deviation — as reported in #555.

## Root Cause

The old formula computed the exponentially weighted **root mean square**
of raw values:
````
σ_t = sqrt((1-α)*σ_{t-1}² + α*x_t²)
```

For a sequence like `1..50`, this equals `sqrt(EWMA(x²)) ≈ EWMA(x)`,
which is why the output matched `ewmMean` exactly.

## Fix

The new formula tracks the EWM mean and uses **deviations from the
mean**, giving a proper exponentially weighted standard deviation:

```
mean_t = (1-α)*mean_{t-1} + α*x_t
var_t  = (1-α)*var_{t-1} + α*(x_t - mean_{t-1})²
σ_t    = sqrt(var_t)
````

This matches the formula used by `pandas.ewm(adjust=False).std()` in
structure (without the bias correction that Pandas applies by default).

`ewmCovMatrix` is updated consistently to subtract the EWM mean vector,
preserving the invariant that the diagonal of `ewmCovMatrix` equals
`ewmVar`.

## Changes

- `src/Deedle.Math/Finance.fs`: Fix `ewmVol` and `ewmCovMatrix` formulas
- `tests/Deedle.Math.Tests/Finance.fs`: Add regression test; update
expected `annualVol` from `0.14437` → `0.14553` (the old value was
computed using the incorrect formula applied to stock returns with
non-zero means)

## Test Status

**Build**: ✅ Both `Deedle` and `Deedle.Math` compile successfully.

**Tests**: The test runner requires .NET 5.0 which is not available in
this environment (only .NET 8/9/10 are present — infrastructure issue).
Logic was validated by running an F# script directly against the
compiled DLLs:
- `ewmVol` on `1..50` with span=10 → **5.50** (old formula: 45.5 = same
as mean) ✅
- Diagonal consistency (`ewmVar` vs diagonal of `ewmCovMatrix`) → diff =
**~1e-19** ✅

Closes #555

commit.message:
Use Domain instead of Thread for Eval (#12752)

* add sctx.pool

* Fix pool usage, enable parallel analyzer, fix data races

- Add Parallel.run_with_pool to wrap operations in Task.run (fixes
  Unhandled(Wait) exception from missing effect handler)
- Update all call sites to use run_with_pool instead of bare Lazy.force
- Enable parallel analyzer by default (remove opt-in gate)
- Make apply_params_stack domain-local via Domain.DLS (fixes data race)
- Make alloc_mid use Atomic (consistency with alloc_var)
- Protect DCE CfMaybeUsed writes with field_marker_mutex (fixes flag race)

Co-authored-by: Simn <634365+Simn@users.noreply.github.com>

* move apply_params_stack to uctx

* kinda works

* add DomainMutex

* Fix DomainMutex data race and thread ID generation race

The DomainMutex.downer field was a non-atomic mutable record field read by
multiple domains without synchronization. In OCaml 5, this is a data race
that can cause a domain to see a stale ownership value, incorrectly thinking
it owns the mutex. Fixed by using Atomic.t for the owner ID field.

Also fixed thread ID generation which used non-atomic Atomic.incr + Atomic.get
pattern. Replaced with atomic Atomic.fetch_and_add.

Co-authored-by: Simn <634365+Simn@users.noreply.github.com>

* pull changes from sub-PR

* investigate if we're actually looking at the problem

* revert

* focus

* focus more

* pull luv changes to get that out of the way

* pull ThreadSafeHashtbl changes because that's a clear bugfix

* pull Deque changes because that's simpler

* use normal mutex for condition because it doesn't have to be reentrant

* enable hanging test again to see how it behaves on other OS

* fix thread id in debug

* don't use Sys.time for Lock.wait

* Replace Thread.delay with Unix.sleepf in eval to fix hang in Domain-spawned threads

Thread.delay depends on OCaml's threads library per-domain state
(caml_release_domain_lock/caml_acquire_domain_lock), which may not be
properly initialized for domains created via Domain.spawn on Windows
and macOS. Unix.sleepf uses the OCaml runtime's blocking section API
(caml_enter_blocking_section/caml_leave_blocking_section) which works
correctly for any domain.

Also simplified Sys.sleep to avoid mixing OCaml's Sys.time() (CPU time)
with wall-clock sleep durations, and added safety guards for negative
sleep values.

commit.message:
fix(frontend,feature-svc): critical deposit payment flow mixed-state bug

Root cause: backend reused pending deposits + frontend normalizeNetworkName
always overrode API response with local form state, causing mixed data
(new token/network + old amount) when user went back and resubmitted.

Backend changes (feature-service):
- Add Cancelled status to DepositStatus enum
- Add CancelDepositAsync method + POST cancel endpoint
- Harden OxaPay callback: still credit wallet for cancelled deposits
  if crypto payment arrives (protect user funds)
- Fix reuse logic comment for clarity

Frontend changes:
- Fix normalizeNetworkName: use API value, not local state override
- Fix deposit object construction: 100% from API response, no fallback
  to local form state that caused mixed data
- Lock Step 2: remove back button when invoice is active (standard
  payment gateway pattern - prevents invoice mismatch)
- Add completeness guard: Step 2 won't render with incomplete data
- Add resetDepositState: full state reset (form inputs, timers,
  countdown, depositData) on expired/failed/cancel/deposit-lagi
- Add Batalkan Deposit button with clear warning about sent crypto
- Add Cancelled status label to deposit history

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Add OpenTofu support

- Use fully-qualified provider source registry.terraform.io/harvester/harvester
  in all versions.tf files and examples so OpenTofu does not try to resolve it
  against registry.opentofu.org (where the provider is absent)
- harvester-vm.sh: auto-detect terraform vs tofu binary (prefers terraform,
  falls back to tofu); add --tofu flag and TF_CMD env var override; replace
  all hardcoded 'terraform' calls with $TF_CMD
- README.md: document OpenTofu as an alternative to Terraform, add --tofu flag
  to options table, note TF_CMD env var, add tofu destroy examples
- AGENTS.md: add tofu validate commands, document provider source requirement,
  TF_CMD/--tofu convention, and 'do not shorten provider source' guard

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
